Task description and verifier.py: These keywords align with the fly ash concrete scenario where Portland cement is replaced with fly ash in concrete manufacturing

TRACI 2.1 LCIA methodology (from EPA, verified via web search): TRACI 2.1 includes impact categories for Global Warming (reported as GWP in kg CO2e), Acidification (kg SO2e), and Eutrophication (kg N-eq). Source: https://www.epa.gov/chemical-research/tool-reduction-and-assessment-chemicals-and-other-environmental-impacts-traci

privileged_info_summary: This task uses real, publicly available Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) data. The USLCI database (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database) FY24 Q4 from NREL's Federal LCA Commons is downloaded from GitHub (https://github.com/FLCAC-admin/uslci-content). The LCIA methods use EPA's TRACI 2.1 tool, downloaded from Figshare, which includes the required impact assessment categories: Global Warming Potential (GWP), Acidification, and Eutrophication. The task involves modifying a concrete/cement manufacturing process to replace 30% Portland cement with fly ash, creating a product system, and running LCIA calculations. Output is expected at /home/ga/LCA_Results/fly_ash_concrete_lcia.csv. All metadata file paths are correctly configured in the task setup scripts.
